items.q: What can 'la estación' mean in Spanish?
items.correct: Both 'station' and 'season' — context tells you which.
items.misconception: Only 'station', as in a train station.
items.why: Students learn 'estación = station' first and are confused when it appears meaning 'season of the year'.

items.q: How do you say 'It is hot (weather)' in Spanish?
items.why: English uses 'to be' for weather, so students translate literally with estar instead of the idiomatic hacer.
Students overgeneralise 'en' from en coche and en tren to walking, but going on foot is 'a pie'.
Correct answer: Está lloviendo.. “Hace sol.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's The environment. “The town centre” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Gira a la izquierda.. “Gira a la derecha.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which phrase says 'We should recycle more'?
items.why: Correct answer: Se debería reciclar más.. “Se puede reciclar más.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many of these are means of transport: el tren, el coche, el autobús, la plaza?
items.why: Exact answer: 3 items. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
